title: narcissus abscissus var. serotinus
intro: Narcissus abscissus var. serotinus, also known as the 'wild winter daffodil' or 'narcissus', is a rare and lesser-known species of the narcissus plant, characterized by its single, white or yellowish flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits associated with this plant are said to include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs and flowers are often dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
